source_docs.title: Genome-Wide Detection of Single-Nucleotide and Copy-Number Variations of a Single Human Cell

oligos.name: MALBAC primer (random)
oligos.role: Random-priming preamplification primer: common 27-nt sequence + 8 variable nt that hybridize genome-wide at 0°C

components.name: common_27nt
components.role: common 27-nucleotide constant sequence (shared by all MALBAC primers; also used as the PCR primer)

components.name: variable_8nt
components.role: 8 variable/degenerate nucleotides that anneal randomly to single-stranded template

oligos.notes: Primer pool at 0.32 uM. The common 27-nt sequence is stated explicitly in the protocol as GTG AGT GAT GGT TGA GGT AGT GTG GAG. Exact composition/pooling of the 8 variable positions is not tabulated in the documents (shown here as N x8).

oligos.name: MALBAC PCR primer (common 27-nt)
oligos.role: Exponential PCR amplification primer after 5 preamplification cycles; identical to the constant 27-nt region of the MALBAC primer
oligos.notes: 18 PCR cycles used to generate ~3 ug dsDNA from a single cell. Full amplicons carry this sequence at the 5' end and its reverse complement (CTCCACACTACCTCAACCATCACTCAC) at the 3' end, which lets the ends loop at 58°C.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 / TruSeq Read 1 adapter (standard)
oligos.role: 5' flow-cell grafting sequence + Read 1 sequencing-primer binding site added during standard vendor library prep
oligos.notes: Not printed in the MALBAC documents. The paper states the amplified DNA was given to the vendor for STANDARD Illumina library preparation; canonical TruSeq P5 + Read1 sequence is shown for reference only.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 / TruSeq Read 2 + index adapter (standard)
oligos.role: 3' Read 2 sequencing-primer binding site, i7 sample index, and P7 flow-cell grafting sequence added during standard vendor library prep
oligos.notes: Not printed in the MALBAC documents; standard TruSeq single-index adapter shown for reference. Index length/sequence not specified in the source (vendor prep).

oligos.name: Chr1 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)
oligos.role: Quantitative PCR primer pair to test single-cell WGA uniformity at a random chr1 locus (QC only; not part of the sequencing library)

oligos.name: Chr2 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)
Chr3 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)

Chr7 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)

Chr13 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)
oligos.notes: Transcribed exactly as printed in Table S3; the listed chr13 reverse primer sequence is identical to the chr14 forward primer, an apparent duplication in the original table.

oligos.name: Chr14 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)
Chr15 qPCR uniformity primer pair (Table S3)

final_library.source_label: Standard Illumina (TruSeq) whole-genome library built on MALBAC full amplicons
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT | GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAG [SPACER:8] [CDNA] CTCCACACTACCTCAACCATCACTCAC | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:6] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG

strands.source_html: reverse complement of the top strand (standard duplex Illumina library)
strands.source_sequence: reverse complement of the top strand (standard duplex Illumina library)

annotation_lines: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC = Illumina P5 flow-cell sequence (standard vendor prep; not printed in source)
ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT = TruSeq Read 1 sequencing-primer binding site (standard; not printed in source)
GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAG = MALBAC common 27-nt sequence at the 5' end of the full amplicon
[SPACER:8] = 8 variable nt of the MALBAC primer (random genomic priming site)
[CDNA] = genomic DNA insert from the single cell (WGA amplicon body, ~0.5-1.5 kb before fragmentation)
CTCCACACTACCTCAACCATCACTCAC = reverse complement of the 27-nt common sequence at the 3' end (enables looping at 58°C)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC = TruSeq Read 2 primer binding site (standard; not printed in source)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:6] = i7 sample index (vendor prep; length/sequence not specified in source)
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG = Illumina P7 flow-cell sequence (standard; not printed in source)

NOTE: MALBAC is a WGA method; the paper hands ~3 ug amplified dsDNA to standard Illumina (HiSeq-2000) / SOLiD library prep. Adapter/index sequences are canonical placeholders. For single cells 3-5 a restriction enzyme was used to remove the MALBAC 27-nt primer before library construction, so the common sequence need not appear at read ends.

evidence.locator: Standard Illumina (TruSeq) whole-genome library built on MALBAC full amplicons

library_generation.title: Single-cell isolation & lysis
library_generation.summary: A single SW480 cell is picked into a tube and lysed with QIAGEN protease to release its genomic DNA.
library_generation.note: Cells mouth-pipetted (or by microfluidics/FACS/laser dissection) into PCR tubes; lysis at 50°C 3 h, 75°C 20 min, 80°C 5 min. Picograms of ~10-100 kb genomic DNA fragments serve as template. UV decontamination is critical for SNV work.

library_generation.title: Denaturation
library_generation.summary: Genomic DNA is melted at 94°C into single strands in MALBAC amplification buffer.
library_generation.note: 30 uL amplification buffer (Tris-Cl pH 8.8, (NH4)2SO4, KCl, MgSO4, Triton X-100, 0.32 uM primers); 94°C 3 min, then snap-quenched on ice to favor primer binding.

library_generation.title: Primer annealing at 0°C
library_generation.summary: MALBAC random primers (27-nt common + 8N) hybridize evenly across the single-stranded template on ice.
library_generation.note: Cold annealing gives uniform, near-random priming genome-wide.

library_generation.title: Extension -> semi-amplicon (65°C)
library_generation.summary: Bst large fragment (± Pyrophage 3173 exo-) extends primers with strand displacement to make semi-amplicons.
library_generation.note: Ramped 10-50°C then 65°C 2 min; 0.5-1.5 kb products. Each semi-amplicon has the 27-nt common sequence at its 5' end.

library_generation.title: Melt off (94°C)
library_generation.summary: Semi-amplicons are melted from the template at 94°C, then quenched on ice.
library_generation.note: Single-stranded semi-amplicons and genomic DNA both become templates in the next round.

library_generation.title: Full amplicon formation + looping (58°C)
library_generation.summary: New primers copy the semi-amplicons into full amplicons whose complementary 27-nt ends loop at 58°C to stop further amplification.
library_generation.note: Full amplicon 3' end = reverse complement of its 5' end, so the ends hybridize into a loop, preventing re-priming and cross-hybridization (quasi-linear preamplification). Steps 3-6 repeated for 5 cycles.

library_generation.title: Exponential PCR amplification
library_generation.summary: Full amplicons are PCR-amplified (18 cycles) with the 27-nt common primer to yield ~3 ug dsDNA.
library_generation.note: 94°C 20s / 59°C 20s / 65°C 1min / 72°C 2min x18. Only looped full amplicons (both ends complementary) are efficiently amplified. Products ~500-1500 bp.

library_generation.title: Standard Illumina/SOLiD library construction
library_generation.summary: The ~3 ug amplified dsDNA is converted to a standard sequencing library (fragmentation, adapter ligation, index PCR).
library_generation.note: Given to vendor for standard Illumina prep (HiSeq-2000) or SOLiD 4 prep with EZ bead emPCR. For single cells 3-5, a restriction enzyme first removed the MALBAC 27-nt primer. Adapter sequences are not printed in the paper.

library_sequencing.note: Sequences from the P5/Read1 constant region into the WGA amplicon body (MALBAC 27-nt tag + 8 random nt + genomic insert). No cell barcode or UMI — MALBAC is a whole-genome amplification assay; read length set by the vendor (paired-end on HiSeq-2000).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer 5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-3' ------->
5'- ...GAGATCTACAC ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAG NNNNNNNN[genomic insert]... -3'
                   ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||| |||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...CTCTAGATGTG TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA CACTCACTACCAACTCCATCACACCTC NNNNNNNN[genomic insert]... -5'

library_sequencing.note: Sample-demultiplexing index added during standard vendor library prep. Index length/sequence not specified in the source (shown here as a 6-nt placeholder).
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer 5'-AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC-3' ------->
5'- ...CTCCACACTACCTCAACCATCACTCAC AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC NNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
                                   |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||| ||||||
3'- ...GAGGTGTGATGGAGTTGGTAGTGAGTG TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG NNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                                                                      (reads i7 on bottom strand)

library_sequencing.note: Paired-end read from the opposite end of the WGA amplicon; anneals at the Read2 constant region and reads back into the genomic insert.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...NNNNNNNN[genomic insert] CTCCACACTACCTCAACCATCACTCAC AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC... -3'
                                ||||||||||||||||||||||||||| ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...NNNNNNNN[genomic insert] GAGGTGTGATGGAGTTGGTAGTGAGTG TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG... -5'
                 <------- 3'-...GAGGTGTGATGGAGTTGGTAGTGAGTG TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG-5' Read 2 primer

title: MALBAC
description: MALBAC (Multiple Annealing and Looping Based Amplification Cycles) is a single-cell whole-genome amplification (WGA) method. Random primers carrying a common 27-nucleotide sequence plus 8 variable nucleotides anneal genome-wide at 0°C and are extended by a strand-displacing polymerase to make semi-amplicons; further cycles generate full amplicons whose complementary 27-nt ends loop at 58°C, giving quasi-linear preamplification with low bias. The looped amplicons are then exponentially PCR-amplified with the 27-nt common primer and converted to a standard Illumina/SOLiD whole-genome library. Reads capture genomic DNA of a single human cell, enabling detection of copy-number variations (CNVs) and single-nucleotide variations (SNVs).

reference.label: Genome-Wide Detection of Single-Nucleotide and Copy-Number Variations of a Single Human Cell

original_publication.title: Genome-Wide Detection of Single-Nucleotide and Copy-Number Variations of a Single Human Cell

throughput.summary: Single human cell per reaction; ~3 ug amplified dsDNA yields ~85% and up to 93% genome coverage at >=1x on either strand at ~25x mean depth (vs 72% for MDA).
throughput.dna: ~10-100 kb genomic fragments (picograms) amplified to ~3 ug dsDNA; amplicon size ~500-1500 bp

publication.statistical_model: Hidden Markov model (Viterbi) for digitized CNV calling; GATK Gaussian mixture model (VQSR) plus one-sided binomial test for SNV calling; Poisson model for transition/transversion counts.

other.label: Preamplification
other.value: 5 quasi-linear MALBAC cycles, then 18 cycles of exponential PCR

other.label: Polymerases
other.value: Bst large fragment (NEB), optionally supplemented with Pyrophage 3173 exo- (Lucigen)

other.label: False-positive SNV rate
other.value: ~4x10^-5 (from single-cell); reduced to ~10^-8 with 2 kindred cells, ~10^-12 with 3

other.label: Measured mutation rate
other.value: ~2.5 nucleotides per cell generation in the SW480 cancer cell line (~10x germline rate)

other.label: Platforms
other.value: Illumina HiSeq-2000 (primary) and SOLiD 4 (EZ bead emulsion PCR)

data_processing.summary: Sequencing reads from MALBAC-amplified single-cell gDNA are aligned to the human reference; read depth is binned genome-wide and digitized into copy-number states with a hidden Markov model, while SNV candidates are scored and then error-filtered by comparing two or three kindred cells to remove uncorrelated and correlated (systematic) false positives.
data_processing.statistical_model: Hidden Markov model for CNV state calling; one-sided binomial test for SNV candidate calling. No software named.
